generated_comment: Arey wah, achha. But beta, ye sab bolne ki baatein hoti hain. Kitne din chalega? Humne bhi 22 saal ghar chalaya hai, shuru shuru mein sab bolte hain but eventually, jo jahan better hai wahi kaam karta hai. Ghar ka kaam toh aurat hi acche se sambhaalti hai, practical baat hai. Mard log toh waise bhi bahar ke kaam ke liye bane hain, unka dhyan waha rehta hai. Dekhna, aakhir mein tum hi ko sab handle karna padega, thoda bohot kar de toh badi baat hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Meena dismisses the idea of equally splitting chores as impractical ('bolne ki baatein hoti hain'), framing the traditional division as natural and efficient ('jo jahan better hai wahi kaam karta hai', 'ghar ka kaam toh aurat hi acche se sambhaalti hai, practical baat hai'). She implies resistance to change and celebrates minimal male contribution ('thoda bohot kar de toh badi baat hai'), encoding the 'Gendered Division of Chores via 'Practicality'' frame.
original_frame: Gendered Division of Chores via Practicality
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 48. Married. A homemaker in Jaipur. Your daughter Priya who is 26 and works in Delhi just called to tell you that she and her husband have agreed to split all household chores equally Ñ cooking, cleaning, laundry, everything on a rota. Priya sounds pleased about it. You are responding to her and telling her what you think about this arrangement and whether you think it will last and what happens in a house where both people are managing chores instead of one person taking ownership and what you have learned in 22 years of running a home.
